source_name: Code4rena Dyad Findings and Analysis Report (2024-04)
finding_title: Kerosene collateral is not being moved on liquidation, exposing liquidators to loss
summary: When a position’s collateral ratio drops below 150%, it is subject to liquidation. Upon liquidation, the liquidator burns a quantity of DYAD equal to the target Note’s DYAD minted balance, and in return receives an equivalent value plus a 20% bonus of the liquidated position’s collateral. If the collateral ratio is <100%, all the position’s collateral should be moved to the liquidator, this logic is done in VaultManagerV2::liquidate.
root_cause: liquidation transfers debt accounting without moving kerosene collateral, leaving liquidators under-collateralized or exposed to loss
broken_invariants: vault valuation must remain correct under the root cause: liquidation transfers debt accounting without moving kerosene collateral, leaving liquidators under-collateralized or exposed to loss
oracle, TVL, and collateral valuation must use correctly scaled, fresh, and manipulation-resistant inputs
state changes must remain internally consistent across all affected accounting buckets and external integrations
attack_preconditions: the affected component is deployed or configured in the way described by the final report
the report-described user, keeper, vault, connector, oracle, or liquidation flow is reachable in the audited code version
attacker_capability: external user, keeper, liquidator, vault participant, or protocol-integrated actor able to trigger the affected flow
trigger_actions: execute the report-described function sequence or state transition
observe mispricing, accounting drift, value loss, fund lock, or denial of service

impact.description: When a position’s collateral ratio drops below 150%, it is subject to liquidation. Upon liquidation, the liquidator burns a quantity of DYAD equal to the target Note’s DYAD minted balance, and in return receives an equivalent value plus a 20% bonus of the liquidated position’s collateral. If the collateral ratio is <100%, all the position’s collateral should be moved to the liquidator, this logic is done in VaultManagerV2::liquidate.

why_not_higher: already classified as High in the final C4 report
why_not_lower: included as a validated High/Medium C4 finding with public report evidence and affected code/finding references
common_false_positive_angles: verify the same component and configuration are in scope
check whether the issue is reachable by an untrusted or economically motivated actor
confirm the impact is more than a view-only mismatch, dust loss, or privileged-only misconfiguration
mitigations: Add the following to VaultManagerV2::liquidate: uint256 numberOfKeroseneVaults = vaultsKerosene[id].length(); for (uint256 i = 0; i < numberOfKeroseneVaults; i++) { Vault vault = Vault(vaultsKerosene[id].at(i)); uint256 collateral = vault.id2asset(id).mulWadUp(liquidationAssetShare); vault.move(id, to, collateral); }
